description: Apply dilation to rblock surfaces, expanding or contracting facet positions.

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: Use itasca.command('rblock dilate ...') via command interface

keywords.name: keep-inertial
keywords.description: Do not adjust the inertial properties as a result of dilation. This may be advantageous when the rounding distance is small.

keywords.name: relative
keywords.description: The degree of rounding is computed as the product of f and the radius of a {circle in 2D; sphere in 3D} with the same size as the rigid block.

keywords.name: adummy
keywords.description: The degree of rounding is f .

keywords.name: relative
keywords.description: Dilate the core shape by f without rounding. By default this is an absolute distance, so all core shapes are dilated by the same amount. The amount of expansion is computed as the product of f and the radius of a {circle in 2D; sphere in 3D} with the same size as the rigid block. Thus the expansion varies per rigid block.
